scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from blackmail (sycophancy) had direct material consequences in Greek law, preventing property loss and fines.
scores.generality_reasoning: Sycophancy was a foundational axis of risk in classical civic and economic life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The resource is tied to the state of being poor, which can change via windfall or inheritance.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth brackets are changeable with effort or luck, though often durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch describes social realities and risks prevalent in the Greco-Roman world of his time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a logical social fact, though framed with philosophical observation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The rule is tautological: a blackmiller targets wealth; having no wealth (poor) defines the exemption.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political envy led to ostracism or trials with material costs, but the resource itself is social status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the recurring risk of political prosecution in city-state life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as one avoids office; office-holding was usually annual or term-limited.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by the episodic role of 'not holding office'.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to the enduring structure of Greek city-state politics known to the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Presents a causal sociological observation of the time as factual.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Holding no office does not linguistically define 'envy exemption'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Land ownership is a tangible property entitlement and a primary source of wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis in the ancient world.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Land ownership and citizenship-based exclusion were lifelong structural realities.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Foreigner/Metic status was a structural legal birth-status, very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The verbatim refers to Agesilaus and Tissaphernes, centuries before Plutarch's time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The reasoning accurately identifies the underlying factual legal exclusion of foreigners from land.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a foreigner does not inherently mean one cannot own land; it is a specific legal rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Luxury foods are concrete physical goods with immediate nutritional and social value.
scores.generality_reasoning: Resource is specific to a particular event and type of foodstuff.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A one-time distribution of perishable goods.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Helots were a genetically and birth-fixed hereditary slave class.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the Persian Wars/Spartan Hegemony period, long before the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific historical command and distribution as a past fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Access to luxury foods is not part of the definition of a Helot.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from labor is a concrete legal right that frees the body for other pursuits.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad prohibition spanning all forms of manual/commercial labor.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong structural prohibition for Spartan citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Spartan citizenship was a structural birth-status, though loss of status (Hypomeiones) was possible.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the classical Lycurgan system, historical by Plutarch's time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the prohibition as a clear legal 'custom' or law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not conceptually imply an exemption from manual calling.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Agricultural labor involves physical exertion and control over the means of subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad class of labor covering the entire agricultural sector.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong structural obligation for the Helot class.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Helots were a hereditary, fixed ethnicity/caste.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the classical Spartan kleros system, long defunct in Plutarch's era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the systemic handover of fields as a historical reality.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of Helot (serf) is close, but 'agricultural labor' is the specific instantiation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Giving up a seat is a ceremonial honor with very minor material side-effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly tied to a specific social etiquette in public spaces.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource is attached to the life stage of old age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age (at a given moment) is biologically fixed/immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the legendary/historical 'custom' of the early Spartans.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the 'custom' as a known factual practice of the group.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being elderly does not grammatically mean 'getting a seat'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to athletic training involves use of facilities and physical development.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the institution of the gymnasium and athletic festivals.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage of 'maidens' (pre-marriage or youth).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the Lycurgan institutions of the deep past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the institution of strenuous exercise as a fact of the lawgiver.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman has no conceptual link to wrestling or discus throwing.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Money and currency (gold/silver) are foundational material assets.
scores.generality_reasoning: Wealth in noble metals is a foundational societal axis of power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A lifelong structural prohibition for all residents of the state.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Applies to Spartans, a structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the ancient monetary reforms of Lycurgus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the decree and ordinance as historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a Spartan does not define one as an owner of iron rather than silver.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Kingship provides command over military and religious resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Kingship is a broad category of absolute political/legal power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Hereditary kingship is a lifelong structural role.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by blood descent from specific lineages (Agiads/Eurypontids).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to Lysander's era (late 5th/early 4th C BCE) and the older royal system.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the existing hereditary rule while proposing its abolition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Blood descent exists independently of the legal right to rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Land allotments are tangible property providing subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property distribution is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Allotments (Kleroi) were intended as lifelong/hereditary shares.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship was a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Attributed to the legendary lawgiver Lycurgus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the redistribution as a simple factual event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define the size or equality of one's land.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political influence is a social role that leads to material outcomes (war, money).
scores.generality_reasoning: Influence over state decisions is a broad political resource.
scores.persistence_reasoning: High-status women held this influence throughout their adult lives.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sex/Gender is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the Persian Wars (Gorgo daughter of Cleomenes).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific historical interaction as evidence of social standing.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman does not conceptually entail political influence.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Authority over husbands (men) allows for the control of domestic material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad authority spanning domestic and social behavior.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lasts for the duration of marriage and motherhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sex/Gender is fixed; Spartan identity is structural.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to classical Spartan reputation in contrast to Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a recognized social norm ('lording it over') through a specific dialogue.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Womanhood does not define domestic dominance.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Bearing heirs is a social role with long-term survival and property implications.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational role in lineage and state continuity.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Once children are born, the status as mother of heirs is lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is fixed; status as a King's wife is durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The dialogue is between Gorgo and Leonidas at Thermopylae.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A king's instruction/advice that reflects a deep cultural rule.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a wife does not define the production of 'good children'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Power of life and death is the most concrete material force possible.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to military code enforcement and domestic homicide.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Action relates to a single episode of cowardice.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender and ethnicity ('of Sparta') are fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical anecdote from Sparta's military past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the event as an instantiation of 'our laws'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Motherhood does not include the power to execute offspring.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Autonomous sexual choice is a form of bodily integrity and control.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to sexual/romantic negotiation and marital status.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies for the duration of daughterhood and then marriage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to traditional Spartan social mores.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the structure of obedience as an established 'practice' of the group.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman does not define one as a subject of male mediation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Bodily protection from degrading/unfitting treatment is concrete and material.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the scope of master-slave commands.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Slave status and lack of protection were lifelong conditions.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status was a structural legal status, very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to anecdotes from the classical period of Spartan slave-taking.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the condition of slavery and its lack of protection as a fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: While slave/free are opposites, 'unfitting treatment' is a specific material lack, not the definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to engage in creative professional activity with social standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a single literary genre (comedy).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The prohibition holds as long as one is a member of the council (usually lifelong).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Membership in the Areopagus was a lifelong structural role of the elite.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the classical Athenian legal code.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific statute ('a law forbidding').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an Areopagite does not define one as not-a-comedy-writer.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The choregia required the expenditure of tangible wealth and estates.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the institution of liturgical funding for festivals.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The depletion of wealth from this obligation was a long-term outcome.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealthy status is durable but not immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the classical Athenian choregia system.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the legal outcome of the choregoi's financial loss as common fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being wealthy does not entail an obligation to fund theaters.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public burial is a concrete state expense and symbolic honor.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to funeral rites and location.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A permanent final state and memorial.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by the episodic/final role of dying in battle.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to classical Athenian public funeral customs.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the distinction in who receives burial as an observed state fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship or death does not define a 'public' vs 'private' burial.
